plan_adherence_notes: All 7 plan steps executed faithfully. Step 1 mock refactoring matches spec exactly (replaced @/lib/api/client with @/lib/query/hooks, mocked react-router-dom and session context, created defaultMutationState helper, added beforeEach). Step 2 existing 6 render tests preserved and passing. Steps 3-6 delivered all 16 new tests as specified: 5 validation, 5 interaction, 3 mutation state, 3 submission/navigation. Step 7 full suite passes (22/22). One minor pragmatic deviation: submission tests use fireEvent.submit(form) instead of fireEvent.click(button) to work around jsdom datetime-local constraint validation — a smart adaptation, not a plan violation.
missed_questions: How does jsdom handle datetime-local inputs and HTML5 constraint validation? (caused the fireEvent.submit workaround in submission tests)
Should the liquidity default value (hardcoded 10000) be tested explicitly?
Should the description field's optional nature be tested (submit with empty description)?
reason: Execution is thorough and faithful to the plan. Test count expanded from 6 to 22, covering validation (disabled button states for empty title/expiresAt/outcome fields + enabled when valid), form interactions (preview text, add/remove outcomes, ID sanitization), mutation state rendering (pending, error with Error instance, fallback error), and submission/navigation (correct payload structure, onSuccess navigation, session-conditional logic). All 22 tests pass. Mock strategy follows the established TradingPanel pattern as researched. The missed questions are minor — the jsdom workaround was handled pragmatically during execution, and the untested edge cases (liquidity default, empty description) are low-risk.
